annotations.label: Cofinality of a poset

anchor.snippet: the cofinality cf( A ) of a partially ordered set A is the least of the cardinalities of the cofinal subsets of A

annotations.note: `Order.cof α` is defined as the infimum of cardinalities of cofinal subsets, exactly this definition.

annotations.label: Cofinality via least ordinal (without choice)

anchor.snippet: The cofinality of a partially ordered set A can alternatively be defined as the least ordinal x such that there is a function from x to A with cofinal image

annotations.note: Mathlib defines cofinality via cardinality, not via least ordinal; `ord_cof_eq` provides a cofinal subset of order type `(Order.cof α).ord` but not this alternative definition.

annotations.label: Cofinality of a directed set

anchor.snippet: Cofinality can be similarly defined for a directed set and is used to generalize the notion of a subsequence in a net

annotations.note: `Order.cof` is defined for any preorder (including directed sets), but the net/subnet generalization is not tied to it in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cofinality with greatest element is 1

anchor.snippet: The cofinality of a partially ordered set with greatest element is 1 as the set consisting only of the greatest element is cofinal

annotations.note: `cof_eq_one_iff` states `cof α = 1 ↔ ∃ x, IsTop x`, and `cof_eq_one` gives `cof α = 1` for `OrderTop α`.

annotations.label: Cofinality of finite ordinal/directed set is 1

anchor.snippet: the cofinality of any nonzero finite ordinal, or indeed any finite directed set, is 1

annotations.note: A nonzero finite ordinal is a successor so `cof_succ`/`cof_eq_one_iff` give 1, but there is no general 'finite directed set has cofinality 1' lemma.

annotations.label: Cofinality of successor ordinal is 1

anchor.snippet: The cofinality of any successor ordinal is 1.

annotations.note: `cof_succ (o) : cof (succ o) = 1` is exactly this.

annotations.label: Cofinal subset contains maximal elements

anchor.snippet: Every cofinal subset of a partially ordered set must contain all maximal elements of that set.

annotations.note: `IsCofinal.mem_of_isMax` states that any maximal element belongs to every cofinal set.

annotations.label: Cofinality of finite poset = number of maximal elements

anchor.snippet: the cofinality of a finite partially ordered set is equal to the number of its maximal elements

annotations.note: No lemma equating the cofinality of a finite poset with its count of maximal elements was found.

annotations.label: Cofinality of bounded subsets poset

anchor.snippet: This is partially ordered under inclusion and the subsets with

annotations.note: `mk_subset_mk_lt_cof`/`mk_bounded_subset` compute cardinalities of bounded subsets but do not state the cofinality of this inclusion poset as an example.

annotations.label: Cofinal subsets of naturals are the infinite ones

anchor.snippet: if and only if it is infinite, and therefore the cofinality of

annotations.note: `cof_nat : cof ℕ = ℵ₀` gives the conclusion; `not_bddAbove_iff_isCofinal` captures cofinal = unbounded in ℕ.

annotations.label: Cofinality of the real numbers

anchor.snippet: The cofinality of the real numbers with their usual ordering is

annotations.note: No `Order.cof ℝ = ℵ₀` lemma exists (`cof_eq_aleph0` needs countability, which ℝ lacks).

annotations.label: Cofinality depends on the order

anchor.snippet: This demonstrates that the cofinality depends on the order; different orders on the same set may have different cofinality.

annotations.note: This is an informal meta-observation with no corresponding formal statement.

annotations.label: Totally ordered cofinal subset yields well-ordered cofinal subset

anchor.snippet: admits a totally ordered cofinal subset, then we can find a subset

annotations.note: `ord_cof_eq` produces a well-ordered cofinal subset for already-well-founded linear orders, not the extraction from an arbitrary totally ordered cofinal subset.

annotations.label: Minimal-cardinality cofinal subsets need not be isomorphic

anchor.snippet: with minimal cardinality (that is, their cardinality is the cofinality of

annotations.note: No formalization of this non-isomorphism observation was found.

annotations.label: Minimal order type cofinal subsets are isomorphic

anchor.snippet: with minimal order type will be order isomorphic

annotations.note: No lemma establishing order-isomorphism of minimal-order-type cofinal subsets was found.

annotations.label: Cofinality of an ordinal

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: that is the order type of a cofinal subset of

annotations.note: `Ordinal.cof o` is defined as the `Order.cof` of any linear order isomorphic to `o`.

annotations.label: Cofinality of a set of ordinals

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: The cofinality of a set of ordinals or any other well-ordered set is the cofinality of the order type of that set.

annotations.note: `cof_type : (typeLT α).cof = Order.cof α` identifies cofinality of a well-ordered set with that of its order type.

annotations.label: Limit ordinal has increasing cofinal sequence

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: indexed strictly increasing sequence with limit

annotations.note: `exists_isFundamentalSeq` gives a strictly increasing (via `IsFundamentalSeq.iSup_eq`) cofinal sequence of length `o.cof.ord`.

annotations.label: Countable limit ordinal has cofinality omega

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: any countable limit ordinal has cofinality

annotations.note: `cof_eq_aleph0_of_isSuccLimit (ho : IsSuccLimit o) (ho' : o < ω₁) : cof o = ℵ₀` is exactly this.

annotations.label: Uncountable limit ordinal cofinality

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: An uncountable limit ordinal may have either cofinality

annotations.note: This existential/illustrative dichotomy is not stated as a formal result.

annotations.label: Cofinality of 0 is 0

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: The cofinality of 0 is 0.

annotations.note: `cof_zero : cof 0 = 0` is exactly this.

annotations.label: Cofinality of successor ordinal is 1

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: The cofinality of any successor ordinal is 1.

annotations.note: `cof_succ (o) : cof (succ o) = 1` is exactly this.

annotations.label: Cofinality of nonzero limit ordinal is regular cardinal

anchor.section: Cofinality of ordinals and other well-ordered sets
anchor.snippet: The cofinality of any nonzero limit ordinal is an infinite regular cardinal.

annotations.note: `isRegular_cof (h : IsSuccLimit o) : IsRegular o.cof`, where `IsRegular` includes infiniteness.

annotations.label: Regular ordinal

anchor.snippet: A regular ordinal is an ordinal that is equal to its cofinality.

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsRegular` for cardinals (infinite, equal to its cofinality), but has no notion of a regular ordinal.

annotations.label: Singular ordinal

anchor.snippet: A singular ordinal is any ordinal that is not regular.

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsSingular` for cardinals, but has no notion of a singular ordinal.

annotations.label: Regular ordinal is initial ordinal

anchor.snippet: Every regular ordinal is the initial ordinal of a cardinal.

annotations.note: Without a regular-ordinal definition, this statement (regular ordinals are `ord` of cardinals) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Limit of regular ordinals is initial but maybe not regular

anchor.snippet: Any limit of regular ordinals is a limit of initial ordinals and thus is also initial but need not be regular.

annotations.note: No formal statement about limits of regular ordinals being initial yet possibly singular was found.

annotations.label: Successor aleph is regular under choice

anchor.snippet: Assuming the axiom of choice,

annotations.note: `isRegular_aleph_add_one (o) : IsRegular (ℵ_ (o + 1))` is exactly that successor alephs are regular.

annotations.label: Cofinality is idempotent (regular)

anchor.snippet: is a regular ordinal, that is, the cofinality of the cofinality of

annotations.note: `cof_ord_cof (o) : o.cof.ord.cof = o.cof` is exactly the idempotency/regularity of cofinality.

annotations.label: Cofinality of an infinite cardinal

anchor.snippet: is the least cardinal such that there is an unbounded function from

annotations.note: Mathlib deliberately expresses a cardinal's cofinality as `c.ord.cof` rather than a separate definition (per the file's implementation notes).

anchors.snippet: is the least cardinal such that there is an unbounded function from

anchor.snippet: is also the cardinality of the smallest set of strictly smaller cardinals whose sum is

annotations.label: Cofinality of totally ordered set is regular

anchor.snippet: The cofinality of any totally ordered set is regular

annotations.note: `Order.cof_ord_cof` proves idempotency for well-founded linear orders, but regularity of the cofinality of an arbitrary totally ordered set is not stated.

anchors.snippet: The cofinality of any totally ordered set is regular

annotations.label: König's theorem inequalities for cofinality

anchor.snippet: Using Kőnig's theorem , one can prove

annotations.note: `lt_power_cof_ord : c < c ^ c.ord.cof` and `lt_cof_ord_power : a < (b ^ a).ord.cof` are the König cofinality inequalities.

annotations.label: Cofinality of the continuum is uncountable

anchor.snippet: the cofinality of the cardinality of the continuum must be uncountable

annotations.note: `lt_cof_ord_power` with `a = ℵ₀, b = 2` yields `ℵ₀ < (2^ℵ₀).ord.cof`, but there is no dedicated lemma for the continuum's cofinality.

anchors.snippet: the cofinality of the cardinality of the continuum must be uncountable
